mission.title: node-exporter: Feature: Include OS release/version metric
mission.description: Feature: Include OS release/version metric. Requested by 11+ users.

steps.title: Check current node-exporter deployment
steps.description:
Verify your node-exporter version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n node-exporter -l app.kubernetes.io/name=node-exporter
helm list -n node-exporter 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working node-exporter installation.

steps.title: Review node-exporter configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant node-exporter configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n node-exporter -l app.kubernetes.io/name=node-exporter
kubectl get configmap -n node-exporter -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=node-exporter
```
Currently Node Exporter has a metric called: node_uname_info which of course exposes uname info. While this is nice, it does not help if you are running different OSes which could have similar uname info.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Feature: Include OS release/version metric
steps.description:
I agree with the statement. Also these values won't change a lot, so to achieve this, I added this simple textfile collector script executed via `cron` at reboot updating the value (in case if it changes). Let me know if this serves the purpose, I can check and create a PR on
```yaml
#!/bin/bash

TEXTFILE_COLLECTOR_PATH=/var/lib/node_exporter/textfile_collector
. /etc/os-release

cat << INFO > ${TEXTFILE_COLLECTOR_PATH}/os-release.prom.xx
# HELP node_os_info A metric with a constant '1' value labeled by id, id_like, name, pretty_name, version, version_codename and version_id
# TYPE node_os_info gauge
INFO
echo "node_os_info{id=\"${ID}\",id_like=\"${ID_LIKE}\",name=\"${NAME}\",pretty_name=\"${PRETTY_NAME}\",version=\"${VERSION}\",version_codename=\"${VERSION_CODENAME}\",version_id=\"${VERSION_ID}\"} 1" >> ${TEXTFILE_COLLECTOR_PATH}/os-release.prom.xx

mv ${TEXTFILE_COLLECTO
```

steps.title: Upgrade node-exporter to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade node-exporter:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade node-exporter node-exporter/node-exporter --namespace node-exporter
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n node-exporter
helm list -n node-exporter
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n node-exporter -l app.kubernetes.io/name=node-exporter
kubectl get events -n node-exporter --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Feature: Include OS release/version metric" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: I agree with the statement. Also these values won't change a lot, so to achieve this, I added this simple textfile collector script executed via `cron` at reboot updating the value (in case if it changes). Let me know if this serves the purpose, I can check and create a PR on https://github.com/prometheus-community/node-exporter-textfile-collector-scripts for the same.
codeSnippets:
#!/bin/bash

TEXTFILE_COLLECTOR_PATH=/var/lib/node_exporter/textfile_collector
. /etc/os-release

cat << INFO > ${TEXTFILE_COLLECTOR_PATH}/os-release.prom.xx
# HELP node_os_info A metric with a constant '1' value labeled by id, id_like, name, pretty_name, version, version_codename and version_id
# TYPE node_os_info gauge
INFO
echo "node_os_info{id=\"${ID}\",id_like=\"${ID_LIKE}\",name=\"${NAME}\",pretty_name=\"${PRETTY_NAME}\",version=\"${VERSION}\",version_codename=\"${VERSION_CODENAME}\",version_id=\"${VERSION_ID}\"} 1" >> ${TEXTFILE_COLLECTOR_PATH}/os-release.prom.xx

mv ${TEXTFILE_COLLECTOR_PATH}/os-release.prom.xx ${TEXTFILE_COLLECTOR_PATH}/os-release.prom

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with node-exporter installed or the issue environment reproducible.
